title: John Chiarelli
entries: Although he is very kind, his teaching needs work. Be prepared to teach yourself taking this class as he expects you to know all the material by the next class and quizzes you on it every single class. On top of this he assigns a ton of homework. He also goes over lecture time by at least 10 minutes every day. Definitely over complicates things.
This is a hard course but he over complicates the work. You will have a quiz every class period.

You will have to teach yourself. He over complicates everything. Go on Youtube and watch The Chemistry Tutor or someone else. He gives out quizzes every day. Half the class is him doing problems of the HW, then you take a quiz and after that he teaches for 30-45 mins. The class was 1:45 mins. No formulas given on tests. Miss a class you miss a quiz

Textbook is extremely dry. Make sure to attend lectures, attend office hours, and email him with any questions. He always answered me within a day or two, and genuinely cared. Kindest professor I've ever had at NYU. He knew everyone's name by the end of the first week

Took this class expecting the worst as a result of the prior ratings. Not going to lie, Professor Chiarelli was extremely kind. He had everyone's name memorized by the 2nd lecture. Yes, attendance is important as there were pop quizzes. Difficult content, but he was always accessible in office hours and answered emails within 48 hours. Great prof!

I took his discrete class and it was not as bad as the rating suggests. Prof. Chiarelli is caring in class, and he is accessible during office hours. Discrete itself is not an easy A class and you do need to put in efforts to get a good grade. The quizzes and exams are hard, but they were still doable.
trust me this professor is completely .... .

Professor Chiarelli is incredibly caring; for example, after the first class, he already knew all our names. He is very smart and passionate about teaching, cracks jokes in class, and always takes time to answer everyone's questions. The course material is very dense, and there is a lot of homework, but you certainly learn a lot!

Not nearly as bad as ratings would suggest. Very passionate and knowledgeable about mathematics, will give thorough answers to any questions. Mostly explains concepts well and class is very interactive. Homeworks are somewhat intensive and more difficult than lecture material, but very doable.
Worst professor I have ever met! This introductory course makes you doubt your ability to do math even if you do well in advanced math courses and possess mathematical maturity.
Worst professor in the world !!!!!!!
Dr. Chiarelli has the ability to teach math in the same way that I have the ability to breathe underwater. His complete inability to explain even the simplest concepts is astounding.
Super tough grader! Average of class is horrible. Attending lecture and reading textbook is not enough (I have no clue what's enough)! Horrible experience. Worst prof I have ever had in NYU.

Worst professor I've met even. He couldn't cover all the contents so he increased homework largely AFTER MIDTERM 1!!! Never release answers for hw, quizzes, and any task he assigned, students don't know why they got wrong. The average for exams and quizzes is C or C-. You must rely on self-study since his lectures are fruitless. Curves unknown:)
So many pop quizzes. He grades super hard and is picky at everything. Why is he a professor? He cannot teach. I solely relied on the textbook.

Unprofessional. His midterm and final were full of typing errors which he fixes midway through the exam, as if he never actually looked at them. He couldn't cover the material needed in the course, so he dumped it all in the last week. Doesn't give homework answers, sometimes it took him months to return homework/exam grades. Run away.

This class was the most torturous exercise in learning math I've ever seen. It made me lose faith in the field of mathematics as a whole. Dr. Chiarelli should be ashamed for the way he taught this class.

Lots of quizzes. Horrible professor. Not good at teaching. Cannot explain anything clearly. Fully survived on reading the textbook. Midterms and finals are ridiculously difficult and the average is super low. If someone wants to challenge their mental state, take him, or else, run.

Worst math professor in Courant. He never gives solutions to quizzes assignments and exams. Very unclear about topics that would be on the exams/quizzes. Never reply to emails. Lectures long, boring and unorganized. Takes forever to get the score back. Textbook hard to read. Super tough when grading exams. No idea about curves. Exams and quiz hard

WORST PROFESSOR EVER! MOST CHAOTIC CLASS EVER! He literally reads off the textbook during lectures and asks the students to teach by questioning them. Homeworks, quizzes, and tests are difficult and graded harshly. No answers key to homework, quizzes, and test so you never know what you get wrong and how you can improve. Quiz times are random too.
